name: Biscuits
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon vegetable shortening (the size of a small egg)
recipeInstructions:
1. Use enough buttermilk to make the dough sticky yet workable for kneading and rolling out.
2. Mix the ingredients together, gradually adding buttermilk.
3. Turn the dough onto a floured surface and knead additional self-rising flour into it, being careful not to handle the dough too much.
4. Roll the dough until it is about 1/4 inch thick.
5. Fold the dough over to double its size and gently press on it. This fold helps the biscuits separate easily after baking.
6. Cut biscuits using a floured glass, approximately 2-1/2 inches in diameter. Re-roll the dough as needed.
7. Place the biscuits closely together in an ungreased baking pan for higher rise during baking. Some bakers place a small amount of vegetable oil at one end of the pan and brush the tops of the biscuits with it before placing them in the other end for a crisper, browner top.
8. Bake at 450°F until lightly browned (about 15 minutes). Serve with butter, jelly, honey, or fillings like bacon or sausage. Approximately 1 cup of flour yields about 4-5 biscuits. Increase the recipe as desired, always using one tablespoon of vegetable shortening for each cup of flour.
9. Do not be discouraged if they do not turn out perfectly on the first attempt; practice is key. Use fresh ingredients.
